RED: validate-kaola-workflow-contracts.js (codex chain) — new #572 phase-ban asserted against the unmodified, still-phase-laden templates. `node scripts/validate-kaola-workflow-contracts.js` exits 1 with: Error: commands/workflow-init.md: injected ## Kaola-Workflow template must not teach a numbered Phase <n> model (#572 — adaptive is the unconditional default) (at validate-kaola-workflow-contracts.js:500). Root cause: the KW-CLAUDE-TEMPLATE region still carries "Phase boundaries: Phase 1 … Phase 2 … Phase 3", "In Phase 1 …", "In Phase 4 …" and the "phase files" / "current phase file" durable-state framing across all 6 init surfaces. <!-- GREEN: paste GREEN here -->
GREEN: After re-grounding all 6 init surfaces on the adaptive DAG-of-roles model and moving the durable-state needles, every validator passes: `node scripts/validate-kaola-workflow-contracts.js` → "Kaola-Workflow Codex contract validation passed" (exit 0; the #572 phase-ban + cross-forge content parity now hold over all 6 `initFiles`); `node scripts/validate-workflow-contracts.js` → "Workflow contract validation passed" (exit 0); gitlab + gitea forge validators → "…contract validation passed" (exit 0 each); `node scripts/test-opencode-edition.js` → "opencode-edition test passed (477 assertions)" including the new A24 opencode phase-ban + runtime-noun parity; the #274 byte-mirror pair (scripts/ vs plugins/kaola-workflow/scripts/ validate-workflow-contracts.js) is byte-identical; `node scripts/simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js` exit 0.

## Work summary
- **6-surface rewrite (AC1-AC3, AC6):** the injected `## Kaola-Workflow` block (KW-CLAUDE-TEMPLATE region) is now phase-free across `commands/workflow-init.md`, the GitHub/Codex init `SKILL.md`, and the gitlab/gitea command + init SKILL surfaces — mirrored verbatim modulo the single forge-noun line. New model line: "The workflow runs an adaptive, task-shaped DAG of role nodes: the `planner` authors and freezes `workflow-plan.md`, then the executor runs it node-by-node via the running-set scheduler." Roles named by function (read/research → code-explorer/knowledge-lookup; strategy/blueprint → planner/code-architect; execution → tdd-guide test-first OR implementer for refactors/scaffolding/config; gates → code-reviewer/adversarial-verifier; docs → doc-updater). fast/full demoted to an explicit install-time opt-in aside; `fast-summary.md` dropped from the default. Removed: "Phase 1/2/3/4", "phase boundaries", "phase files", "current phase file", "phase artifact", "each phase continues".
- **Needle moves (AC4/AC5 enforcement):** added the #572 phase-number ban (`/Phase\s+\d/`) + phase-file/artifact ban (`/phase file|phase artifact/i`) over the extracted template of all 6 `initFiles`, plus a cross-forge content-parity check (GitHub vs GitLab vs Gitea, normalizing the forge-noun line) in `scripts/validate-kaola-workflow-contracts.js` (codex chain — the one validator that reads all 6 surfaces). opencode phase-ban + runtime-noun parity added as A24 in `scripts/test-opencode-edition.js` (.opencode/ is gitignored, regenerated from canonical via `sync-opencode-edition.js --write`). The init durable-state `assertConcept` in all four validators (claude + its #274 mirror, codex, gitlab, gitea) dropped `fast-summary.md`/bare `.cache/` and added `workflow-plan.md` + `## Node Ledger` + `.cache/{node-id}.md`. The unrelated fast-path `fast-summary.md` needles on the classifier / workflow-next surfaces were left untouched. `scripts/sync-opencode-edition.js` (declared precautionarily) needed no transform change.
